news.headline: Roche Ends Both Huntington's Disease Drug Programs After Biomarkers Failed to Translate to Patient Benefit
news.summary: Roche discontinued both of its Huntington's disease programs after clinical results showed biomarker changes did not translate into patient benefit. This clears a major competitor from the HD field ahead of uniQure's planned Q3 AMT-130 BLA, though it also renews scrutiny of how well surrogate readouts predict clinical outcomes in Huntington's.

news.headline: uniQure jumps ~20% as analysts pile on upgrades and higher targets after Huntington's update
news.summary: Shares surged around 20% as a fresh wave of sell-side upgrades and target hikes followed the AMT-130 regulatory update, with Mizuho lifting its target to $68 and peers clustering in the mid-$60s to $80s. Consensus has swung firmly bullish (Strong Buy, median target ~$71), and retail chatter pushed toward the $150 mark.

news.headline: uniQure (QURE) CEO sells 21,082 shares after option exercise under 10b5-1 plan
news.summary: CEO Matthew Kapusta exercised options at $19.39 and sold 21,082 shares at an average ~$45.16 for about $952K under a pre-scheduled Rule 10b5-1 plan adopted in October 2025, leaving him with 519,227 shares. The pre-planned nature blunts the timing signal, but continued executive selling into the post-FDA rally remains a mild governance watch-item.

news.headline: uniQure Announces Closing of Upsized $259 Million Public Offering
news.summary: uniQure closed roughly 5.69M shares at $45.50 for about $259M gross after underwriters fully exercised the overallotment. Proceeds fund AMT-130 commercialization readiness, a potential launch, and the confirmatory study, de-risking the balance sheet ahead of the costly BLA phase while adding some dilution.

news.headline: uniQure Announces Preliminary Data on the First Cohort in the Phase I/IIa Clinical Trial of AMT-260 in Refractory Mesial Temporal Lobe Epilepsy
news.summary: uniQure reported initial six-month follow-up data on the first, low-dose cohort of six patients in its Phase I/IIa trial of AMT-260, a gene therapy for refractory mesial temporal lobe epilepsy. As of a May 29, 2026 data cutoff, three of the six patients achieved meaningful reductions in disabling seizures during months four through six of follow-up, ranging from 79% to 100% declines versus baseline, an early signal of pipeline diversification beyond Huntington's.

news.headline: uniQure Announces Plan for BLA Submission for AMT-130 in Huntington's Disease
news.summary: In a pivotal Type B meeting the FDA agreed the ~3-year Phase I/II data (75% slowing of cUHDRS progression in the high-dose cohort versus external controls) can serve as the primary basis for a BLA under accelerated approval, reversing prior friction. Submission is planned for Q3 2026 and the stock jumped roughly 78% on the day; AMT-130 holds RMAT, Breakthrough Therapy and Fast Track designations, with a parallel UK MHRA filing also planned.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 earnings (est.)
catalysts.detail: uniQure has not yet set an official date, but based on the Q1 2026 pattern (reported May 5) a Q2 print is expected in late July/early August; watch BLA progress, post-raise cash runway, and commercial-readiness spend.

catalysts.label: AMT-130 BLA submission (FDA)
catalysts.detail: The key driver: uniQure plans to file the BLA for AMT-130 in Huntington's under the accelerated-approval pathway in Q3 2026.

catalysts.label: FDA confirmatory-study design alignment
catalysts.detail: The FDA seeks to pre-align on the required post-approval confirmatory trial before the BLA, potentially using a concurrent standard-of-care control rather than a sham procedure.

catalysts.label: UK MHRA marketing application
catalysts.detail: Parallel marketing authorization application to the UK MHRA based on the same 3-year data, opening a second approval market.

catalysts.label: Potential accelerated approval / PDUFA decision
catalysts.detail: If the BLA is accepted with priority review, an FDA decision on what would be the first-ever Huntington's gene therapy could land in 2027.

pulse: QURE's story flipped completely after the June 17 FDA agreement to accept the ~3-year AMT-130 data (75% slowing of cUHDRS) as the basis for an accelerated-approval BLA, sending shares up ~78% and unleashing a wave of upgrades (Mizuho $68, H.C. Wainwright $80, consensus Strong Buy, median target ~$65-71). Management moved fast to lock in ~$259M via an upsized offering, early AMT-260 epilepsy data (June 19) hinted at pipeline diversification beyond Huntington's, and Roche's July 10 exit from the HD field clears the way for a potential first-mover launch, even as continued CEO/insider selling into the rally and rich valuations warrant caution. As of July 13 no material news has emerged since the July 10 upgrade wave; the setup remains catalyst-rich and binary, hinging on the Q3 2026 BLA filing, the parallel UK MHRA application, and confirmatory-study alignment, with an estimated late-July/early-August Q2 print as the next checkpoint. This trades as a euphoric, event-driven regulatory-momentum story stock, highly volatile rather than fundamentals-driven.
